Document:
# Affairs Officals (IAA) The Affairs Officials (AOs) are considered subordinate to the Upper Management faction they represent.
This means the Affairs Official working for NanoTrasen would be subordinate to the NanoTrasen Representative, while the Affairs Official working for the Concord Frontier Authority would be subordinate to the Magistrate.
Much like their superiors, they are not considered part of Command.
1. Affairs Officials are to represent the best interests of their respective factions in all Space Law matters, and are considered Lawyers for court cases. - For example, if a member of Security was suing Command, or if a tider was suing Security, it would be up to the NT-AO to be the defense, while the CFA-AO would be the prosecution with the Magistrate acting as judge. - Legal matters take priority over Standard Operating Procedure investigations. At no point in time should the Affairs Official neglect court cases over violations of SoP.
2. Affairs Officials may request access to a department to ensure that Space Law or Standard Operating Procedures is being followed. This request does not have to be accepted, however denial may be a cause for concern. - It is recommended to also have the NanoTrasen Representative or Magistrate alongside Affairs Officials performing an inspection to spot Space Law or Standard Operating Procedure violations, if any.
3. Affairs Officials may not significantly impede any department with paperwork, or multiple inspections within a short timeframe.
4. All violations of Standard Operating Procedures or Space Law should be brought to the relevant department head, NT Representative, or Magistrate.
5. Affairs Officials may carry a flash on them at all times.
# Meta Note Until we have the actual AO roles for them, feel free to flip a coin between your fellow IAA to decide what side you represent.
Both of you MUST represent one side or the other. You cannot have two NT-Alligned AOs for example.
